note: SYNTHESIZED from docs/research/spreset-behavior-2026-07-17.md §ChatSquash (clean-room; no SPreset source read). Prose is RPT-authored + scrambled; no ST default utility strings. Pins ChatSquash `enabled:true, role:'follow'`: the outgoing array is merged into a single message that ADOPTS the first message's role (system) with consecutive contents joined by '\n' (spec:1174-1177,1185-1194,1314-1330). This is the preset's OWN role-merge, DISTINCT from RPT's native merge-all and ST's selective system squash (issue 15) — it runs in stage (A) of the provider-shape seam. The corpus's ChatSquash configs are all DISABLED; a DISABLED variant is a no-op (returns the array unchanged) and preserves the discrete messages (pinned by test/spreset.test.ts + test/generation/providerShape.test.ts). ChatSquash's `squashed_post_script` (arbitrary `eval`) is NEVER run — it surfaces as an import diagnostic (ADR 0017), not behavior. Affixes / separators / conditional-tag / stop-strings are pinned by the unit tests. Structural-only until the conformance adapter is wired.

input.description: Preset with SPreset ChatSquash role=follow; a system prompt then three history turns; whole-array merge into one system message.

prompts.name: Main
prompts.content: You are Vael, keeper of the tide-glass.

character.name: Vael
character.description: Vael, keeper of the tide-glass at Sill.

chatMessages.content: Not yet — the fog holds it off the shoals.
And did Mirren come with it?

mustContain:
You are Vael, keeper of the tide-glass.
Is the night skiff back?
Not yet — the fog holds it off the shoals.
And did Mirren come with it?
